skill.name: remix-cache
skill.description: Guidance for implementing Redis-backed caching in Remix applications, including type-safe cache definitions, invalidation, stale-while-revalidate, SSE updates, React hooks, testing, and troubleshooting.
skill.summary: Implement reliable Redis-backed caching patterns for Remix applications.
skill.author: ChaseWNorton

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, filesystem, and script patterns, but review found they are Markdown documentation examples for a Remix caching library. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or executable payload was found. One Redis cleanup command is operationally destructive if copied without care, so the skill is low risk with publication allowed.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution Risk
low_findings.description: The external command findings are documentation snippets and installation paths, not executable scripts in the skill. They are expected for setup and troubleshooting guidance, but users should review commands before running them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is fenced Markdown documentation. TROUBLESHOOTING.md includes a Redis delete pipeline, which is legitimate but can remove cache keys if copied directly.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable and API Key Examples
low_findings.description: The skill shows Redis and API credential placeholders in examples. No evidence shows that the skill reads real secrets or sends them to an unauthorized endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment access appears only in application setup examples. The placeholders are standard Redis and API configuration values, not embedded real credentials.

low_findings.title: Network Calls Are Application Examples
low_findings.description: The fetch and hardcoded URL findings are examples for cached API requests and local SSE testing. They do not establish unauthorized outbound communication by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network operations are inside documented Remix application code. I found no hidden endpoint, encoded payload, or credential exfiltration pattern.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Test Mock Patterns
low_findings.description: The dynamic import findings occur in Vitest examples that import mocked Remix modules. They are not loading attacker-controlled paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import specifiers are static package names used in tests. There is no user-controlled import target or runtime plugin loading behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Redis Cleanup Command
dangerous_patterns.description: A troubleshooting example pipes scanned Redis keys into delete commands. This can remove matching cache entries if a user runs it against the wrong Redis database.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is clearly a documented maintenance example, not malicious code. Confidence is moderate because the operational impact depends on where the user runs it.

dangerous_patterns.title: External API Credential Example
dangerous_patterns.description: An example constructs an external weather API URL with an API key environment variable. This is normal application code, but users should avoid logging full URLs with secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is legitimate for API integrations. The only risk is accidental exposure if a user copies it and logs or shares the resulting URL.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Skill Install Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The README references a Claude skill directory under the user home path. This is installation guidance, not hidden file access by executable code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path appears in a fenced Markdown block explaining where to install the skill. There is no code that reads or writes this path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Injection Search
dangerous_patterns.description: No evidence found for prompt-injection phrases such as override instructions, system update claims, or requests to skip analysis.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: A targeted search across the skill files did not find suspicious instruction override language. The top-level skill text describes normal usage and navigation.

content.value_statement: Remix applications need fast data access without stale or inconsistent user interfaces. This skill guides Redis-backed caching, invalidation, SSE updates, React revalidation, testing, and troubleshooting.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to configure Redis-backed cache instances for Remix server code.
Guides type-safe cache definitions with keys, fetch functions, TTLs, tags, and dependencies.
Covers invalidation by key, tag, pattern, and related cache dependencies.
Documents SSE-based cache events and React hooks for automatic route revalidation.
Provides testing patterns for cache definitions, invalidation logic, and React integration.
Helps troubleshoot Redis connection, stale data, cache stampede, and deployment issues.
limitations: It is documentation guidance, not the remix-cache runtime library itself.
Examples may need adaptation for each project database, Redis provider, and deployment target.
It does not verify live Redis credentials, network access, or production infrastructure settings.
Security and retention policies for cached data remain the application owner's responsibility.

use_cases.title: Add Caching to a Remix App
use_cases.description: Use the skill to create cache definitions, set Redis options, and apply TTL strategies in loaders and actions.

use_cases.title: Keep React Routes Fresh
use_cases.description: Use the skill to wire SSE cache events to React hooks so affected routes revalidate after mutations.

use_cases.title: Harden Cache Behavior Before Launch
use_cases.description: Use the skill to review invalidation, circuit breaker behavior, testing coverage, and Redis troubleshooting steps.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up a First Cache
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add remix-cache to a Remix app with Redis. Show the files I need and explain the basic cache definition.

prompt_templates.title: Design Invalidation Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Remix loaders and actions. Recommend key, tag, and pattern invalidation rules for this data model.

prompt_templates.title: Add Live Revalidation
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through adding SSE cache events and React route revalidation for pages that depend on product and user data.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Cache Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess my remix-cache production plan for Redis failure handling, TTL choices, cache stampede prevention, testing, and observability.

output_examples.input: I need product pages to stay fast but update after inventory changes.
output_examples.output: A recommended cache plan with product keys, inventory tags, mutation-time invalidation, and React revalidation for affected routes.

output_examples.input: My Redis cache sometimes serves stale data after an action.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering invalidation events, tag coverage, SSE connection health, loader dependencies, and test cases.

output_examples.input: Prepare my cache layer for production deployment.
output_examples.output: A review of Redis configuration, TTL defaults, circuit breaker behavior, monitoring events, and cleanup practices.

best_practices: Choose cache keys and tags before writing loaders so invalidation matches real data relationships.
Use short TTLs for volatile data and stale-while-revalidate for expensive reads that tolerate brief staleness.
Test cache hits, misses, invalidation events, and React revalidation before deploying changes.
anti_patterns: Do not place passwords, tokens, or personal data inside cache keys because keys can appear in logs.
Do not use broad pattern invalidation as the only strategy for frequently changed data.
Do not run Redis cleanup commands against shared or production databases without checking the pattern first.

faq.question: What kind of project is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is for Remix applications that use Redis-backed caching and need clear invalidation and revalidation patterns.

faq.question: Does the skill install or run Redis?
faq.answer: No. It explains Redis configuration and troubleshooting, but the user must provide and manage Redis infrastructure.

faq.question: Can it help with React route updates?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents SSE cache events, CacheProvider usage, and hooks that trigger Remix revalidation.

faq.question: Does it support serverless deployments?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation covers serverless mode, Redis-only behavior, and deployment tradeoffs.

faq.question: Are the examples production ready?
faq.answer: They are implementation patterns. Each project should adapt TTLs, error handling, secrets, and monitoring to its own needs.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The documentation includes shell commands, URLs, and environment variable examples. They are expected, but users should review commands and protect secrets.
